steps.title: Identify the appositive phrase structure
steps.content: The phrase a metrical foot ______ to in English metrical verse as a trochee follows a comma after an independent clause. This phrase must be an appositive—a noun phrase that renames or elaborates on a preceding noun—not an independent clause.

steps.title: Choose the form that maintains the noun phrase
steps.content:
A past participle, referred, works as an adjective within the noun phrase: a metrical foot referred to in English metrical verse as a trochee. A conjugated verb form like is referred would create an independent clause, resulting in a comma splice; referring and refers fail idiomatically because each requires a different syntactic structure.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
